channel.title: Cache Invalidation for frontity microservice hosted on AWS
channel.description: @luisherranz I would like to know if there some way I could perform cache invalidation through a configuration. Frontity is deployed as a lambda and API gateway + cloud front is configured. When post is updated or created on the backend, i need to perform cache invalidation manually now everytime. Wordpress service is on another EC2 instance. Could you please share some strategies to avoid this manual process?. Thank you!.

item.title: Cache Invalidation for frontity microservice hosted on AWS
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Hey @vimalkodoth, sorry for the delay.

I would try with a WordPress plugin that does that automatically for you, like https://wordpress.org/plugins/c3-cloudfront-clear-cache/.

Let us know if that works

Read full topic
item.source: Cache Invalidation for frontity microservice hosted on AWS

item.title: Cache Invalidation for frontity microservice hosted on AWS
item.dc:creator: vimalkodoth
item.description:
@luisherranz I would like to know if there some way I could perform cache invalidation through a configuration. Frontity is deployed as a lambda and API gateway + cloud front is configured.
When post is updated or created on the backend, i need to perform cache invalidation manually now everytime. Wordpress service is on another EC2 instance. Could you please share some strategies to avoid this manual process?. Thank you!.

Read full topic
item.source: Cache Invalidation for frontity microservice hosted on AWS
